channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Cuba readies for first Russian oil shipment of the year as energy crisis deepens
item.description: Cuba is preparing to receive its first shipment of Russian oil this year, just days after the government announced it was operating on natural gas, solar power and thermoelectric plants as severe power outages continue to hit it.
item.content:encoded:
Cuba is preparing to receive its first shipment of Russian oil this year, just days after the government announced it was operating on natural gas, solar power and thermoelectric plants as severe power outages continue to hit it.

(Image credit: Ramon Espinosa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: FCC approves merger of local television owners Nexstar and Tegna as two lawsuits seek to block it
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission on Thursday said it had approved the merger of local television giants Nexstar Media Group and rival Tegna, the same day that two lawsuits trying to block the deal were announced.
item.content:encoded:
The Federal Communications Commission on Thursday said it had approved the merger of local television giants Nexstar Media Group and rival Tegna, the same day that two lawsuits trying to block the deal were announced.

(Image credit: Yuki Iwamura)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. Mint can begin to produce Trump commemorative gold coin
item.description: The vote by the U.S. Commission of Fine Arts, whose members are supporters of the Republican president, clears the way for the U.S. Mint to begin production on the coin, whose size and denomination are still under discussion.
item.content:encoded:
The vote by the U.S. Commission of Fine Arts, whose members are supporters of the Republican president, clears the way for the U.S. Mint to begin production on the coin, whose size and denomination are still under discussion.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump is dismantling democracy at 'unprecedented' speed, global report finds
item.description: Three major new studies on democracy and freedom all find the U.S. is slipping further away from democracy. Leaders of two of those studies say President Trump's goal is to rule as an autocrat.
item.content:encoded:
Three major new studies on democracy and freedom all find the U.S. is slipping further away from democracy. Leaders of two of those studies say President Trump's goal is to rule as an autocrat.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: Federal student loans will move to Treasury, further shrinking Education Department
item.description: The Trump administration announced a three-phase transition that will eventually include management of most federal student loans as well as the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA).
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration announced a three-phase transition that will eventually include management of most federal student loans as well as the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA).

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Planned Parenthood chapter settles with EEOC over treatment of white employees
item.description: The Equal Employment Opportunity Commission says Planned Parenthood of Illinois will pay $500,000 to end an investigation that found the organization's DEI practices violated federal civil rights laws
item.content:encoded:
The Equal Employment Opportunity Commission says Planned Parenthood of Illinois will pay $500,000 to end an investigation that found the organization's DEI practices violated federal civil rights laws

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: FDA backs off stricter tanning bed rules with RFK Jr.'s support
item.description: Health officials with the Trump administration have backed away from an effort to more heavily regulate indoor tanning — despite protests from medical groups that warn of the dangers of skin cancer.
item.content:encoded:
Health officials with the Trump administration have backed away from an effort to more heavily regulate indoor tanning — despite protests from medical groups that warn of the dangers of skin cancer.

(Image credit: Adventure_Photo/E+)
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: Trump's mediators offer Hamas formal proposal to give up its weapons in Gaza
item.description: NPR has learned that mediators have quietly given Hamas a proposal to hand over all its weapons to ensure Gaza's reconstruction.
item.content:encoded:
NPR has learned that mediators have quietly given Hamas a proposal to hand over all its weapons to ensure Gaza's reconstruction.

(Image credit: Ahmad Salem)
item.dc:creator: Abu Bakr Bashir

item.title: Afroman prevails in cops' music video defamation suit after a brief but viral trial
item.description: The "Because I Got High" rapper made waves in 2023 with the album and song "Lemon Pound Cake," using home video to mock a police raid on his Ohio home. The deputies lost their civil suit against him.
item.content:encoded: The "Because I Got High" rapper made waves in 2023 with the album and song "Lemon Pound Cake," using home video to mock a police raid on his Ohio home. The deputies lost their civil suit against him.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: 'Movement never lies': 100 years of the Martha Graham Dance Company
item.description: Graham was a creative force in the performing arts. She wanted dance to express authentic, human emotions — a revolutionary idea in the late 1920s.
item.content:encoded:
Graham was a creative force in the performing arts. She wanted dance to express authentic, human emotions — a revolutionary idea in the late 1920s.

(Image credit: Marty Lederhandler)
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Blair
